title: Review the risks of changing Kernel design to be /vote-first in /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public. Identify which executable checks, docs, or launcher behaviors would break or become misleading if /kernel remains co-equal. Return prioritized findings and recommended test set. Do not edit...
summary_text: Best next step: **promote the clean line and preserve the archive as immutable rollback evidence**. 1. Keep `/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public` on `xauto-prod-verify-gha` (`3b2bab652`) as your operational baseline, with no direct feature edits there. 2. Treat `codex/archive-xauto-prod-verify-gha-20260408` (`9499be6c7`) as read-only forensic backup; push it to remote (or export a `git bundle`) so it is not only local `/private/tmp`. 3. Move PR #639 (`codex/kernel-orchestration-hardening-20260408` at `021ea923d`) from draft to ready and merge now, since checks are green and merge state is clean. 4. In the PR description (or merge note), record all three commit anchors (`3b2bab652`, `9499be6c7`, `021ea923d`) and worktree roles; this prevents future ambiguity. 5. After merge, fast-forward local `xauto-prod-verify-gha` to the merge commit, recreate a fresh feature worktree/branch, and keep the archive branch for a defined retention window (for example 14 days) before pruning. If you want, I can give you the exact command sequence for this flow with a “no-footgun” order.
